questions.question: Justify that a quadratic equation cannot have more than two real roots.
questions.answer: The graph of a quadratic equation is a parabola, which can intersect the x-axis at most at two points, so it cannot have more than two real roots.

questions.question: Explain why the discriminant fully determines the nature of roots.
questions.answer: Because it directly indicates whether the square root term in the quadratic formula is real, zero or imaginary.

questions.question: Explain why completing the square is fundamental to the quadratic formula.
questions.answer: The quadratic formula is derived by completing the square on the general quadratic equation.

questions.question: Explain why parabolas model many real-life situations accurately.
questions.answer: Many natural motions follow curved paths that are mathematically parabolic.

questions.question: Explain why roots of quadratic equations represent points of intersection.
questions.answer: Roots represent x-values where the parabola intersects the x-axis.

questions.question: Explain why quadratic equations are central to optimisation problems.
questions.answer: Optimisation problems often involve maximum or minimum values obtained from parabolas.

questions.question: Explain why imaginary roots have no graphical x-intercepts.
questions.answer: Because the parabola does not intersect the x-axis when roots are imaginary.

questions.question: Explain why quadratic equations are polynomial equations of degree two.
questions.answer: They involve the variable raised to the second power and satisfy polynomial conditions.

questions.question: Explain why graphical solutions are approximate in nature.
questions.answer: Because graph drawing and reading depend on scale and accuracy.

questions.question: Explain why quadratic formula is preferred in competitive examinations.
questions.answer: Because it provides a direct and reliable method applicable to all quadratic equations.

questions.question: Explain why the sign of coefficient a affects the end behaviour of a parabola.
questions.answer: The sign of a determines whether the parabola opens upward or downward.

questions.question: Explain why every quadratic equation has exactly two roots (real or complex).
questions.answer: A quadratic equation has two roots which may be real and distinct, real and equal, or complex.

questions.question: Explain the role of symmetry in quadratic graphs.
questions.answer: Parabolas are symmetric about their axis of symmetry.

questions.question: Explain why quadratic equations are used in structural engineering.
questions.answer: They help analyse curves, loads, and stress patterns.

questions.question: Explain why discriminant equal to zero represents equal roots.
questions.answer: Because the square root term becomes zero, giving identical roots.

questions.question: Explain the importance of quadratic equations in calculus.
questions.answer: They form the basis for understanding curves, limits, and optimisation.

questions.question: Explain why negative discriminant has no real-life meaning in some problems.
questions.answer: Because some real-life problems require real solutions only.

questions.question: Explain why quadratic equations are preferred over higher-degree equations in modelling.
questions.answer: They balance simplicity and accuracy.

questions.question: Explain the historical importance of quadratic equations.
questions.answer: They were among the earliest equations studied in algebra.

questions.question: Explain why quadratic equations are fundamental in mathematics education.
questions.answer: They introduce problem solving, algebraic thinking, and graphical interpretation.

questions.question: Explain how quadratic equations help in understanding parabolic motion.
questions.answer: Parabolic motion equations are quadratic in nature.

questions.question: Explain why solving quadratic equations builds analytical thinking.
questions.answer: It requires method selection, reasoning, and interpretation.

questions.question: Explain why quadratic equations are used in business optimisation problems.
questions.answer: They help determine maximum profit and minimum cost.

questions.question: Explain why quadratic equations remain relevant in modern science.
questions.answer: They are used in physics, engineering, economics and data modelling.

questions.question: Explain the overall importance of quadratic equations in mathematics.
questions.answer: Quadratic equations are essential for understanding algebra, graphs, optimisation and real-life modelling.
